project.name: mindmatch4
project.description: React and Vite strategy game hub with Connect Four, Reversi, and Battleship, including adaptive AI, local multiplayer, and offline support.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: HTML shell for the app with metadata, favicons, and the root mount for the React application.

nodes.name: sw.js
nodes.summary: Service worker that caches app assets and supports offline navigation for the deployed site.

nodes.name: vite.config.js
nodes.summary: Vite build configuration that sets the GitHub Pages base path and enables the React plugin.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest defining scripts, dependencies, and project metadata for the MindMatch4 app.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project documentation describing the game hub, development workflow, and deployment expectations.

nodes.name: manifest.json
nodes.summary: Web app manifest for installable PWA behavior, icons, and theme settings.

nodes.name: adapter.js
nodes.summary: Adapter layer that translates board representations and exposes bridge helpers for plugin-style integration.

nodes.name: analytics.js
nodes.summary: Analytics bridge that forwards custom game events to Google Analytics when a measurement ID is configured.

nodes.name: challenge.js
nodes.summary: Produces a deterministic daily seed so Connect Four challenge games can start from a shared position.

nodes.name: coach.js
nodes.summary: High-level AI coach that chooses moves, computes hints, and updates player skill after a game ends.

nodes.name: engine.js
nodes.summary: Core Connect Four search engine implementing board utilities, win detection, and time-boxed negamax search.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses alpha-beta style search primitives and column-major board arrays.

nodes.name: mm4-plugin-wire.js
nodes.summary: DOM wiring layer that attaches hint and daily controls and emits custom MindMatch events for integrations.

nodes.name: mm4-plugin.js
nodes.summary: Exposes the MindMatch AI API on the window object for external use and lightweight in-page tooling.

nodes.name: rating.js
nodes.summary: Stores and updates the player's Elo-like skill rating and maps that rating to AI search parameters.

nodes.name: main.jsx
nodes.summary: React entry point that mounts the root application component and loads global styles.

nodes.name: setup.js
nodes.summary: Client setup code that initializes plugin bridges, registers the service worker, and restores persisted theme state.

nodes.name: styles.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet covering layout, themes, board visuals, overlays, and responsive behavior across the app.

nodes.name: App.jsx
nodes.summary: Top-level screen controller that switches between the home screen and the three game experiences.

nodes.name: BattleshipBoard.jsx
nodes.summary: Battleship board renderer used for both fleet placement and attack targeting views.

nodes.name: BattleshipGame.jsx
nodes.summary: Battleship controller that manages setup, battle turns, AI shooting, tension effects, and end-state handling.

nodes.name: Board.jsx
nodes.summary: Connect Four board presentation component with overlays, focus cues, and hazard indicators.

nodes.name: Game.jsx
nodes.summary: Connect Four game controller responsible for turns, timers, hints, autosave, and AI interaction.

nodes.name: GameTimer.jsx
nodes.summary: Reusable countdown timer component used by all game modes with urgency thresholds and timeout callbacks.

nodes.name: HazardBadge.jsx
nodes.summary: Small UI badge that warns players when a move opens a tactical hazard in Connect Four.

nodes.name: Home.jsx
nodes.summary: Landing screen for choosing game mode, difficulty, sound settings, and player names.

nodes.name: Modal.jsx
nodes.summary: Shared result modal for rematch, sharing, and returning home after a game ends.

nodes.name: ReversiBoard.jsx
nodes.summary: Reversi board renderer with move labels, legal move highlighting, and piece display logic.

nodes.name: ReversiGame.jsx
nodes.summary: Reversi controller that manages valid moves, disc flipping, timers, demo mode, and AI turns.

nodes.name: WinBanner.jsx
nodes.summary: Transient banner overlay that announces the winner and coordinates celebratory feedback.

nodes.name: WinOverlay.jsx
nodes.summary: Connect Four overlay that highlights the winning four-in-a-row line on the board.

nodes.name: battleshipHelpers.js
nodes.summary: Battleship rule engine covering grid creation, ship placement, shot resolution, and AI targeting strategies.

nodes.name: gameHelpers.js
nodes.summary: Connect Four support library providing board helpers, hints, heuristics, sharing text, and procedural audio utilities.

nodes.name: reversiHelpers.js
nodes.summary: Reversi rule helpers for move generation, disc flipping, end-game checks, and heuristic move selection.

nodes.name: battleship.test.js
nodes.summary: Jest suite validating Battleship grid creation, placement rules, shot handling, and AI shot selection.

nodes.name: engine.test.js
nodes.summary: Jest tests for Connect Four winner detection, winning line extraction, and best-move search behavior.

nodes.name: reversi.test.js
nodes.summary: Jest coverage for Reversi move generation, flip application, and corner-preferring AI behavior.

nodes.name: winner
nodes.summary: Evaluates a Connect Four board and returns the winning side or a neutral result.

nodes.name: bestMoveTimeboxed
nodes.summary: Runs bounded negamax search to choose the strongest move within a time budget.
nodes.languageNotes: This function favors stronger moves while respecting a hard time window for interactive play.

nodes.name: aiMove
nodes.summary: Selects an AI move by combining skill parameters, legal moves, and engine search output.

nodes.name: computeHints
nodes.summary: Computes tactical hints such as immediate wins, blocks, and strongest engine suggestions.

nodes.name: updateSkill
nodes.summary: Updates the stored skill rating after a result so later games adapt to the player.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Composes the main application flow and decides which top-level screen to render.

nodes.name: Game
nodes.summary: Runs the Connect Four game loop, including local actions, timers, AI turns, and persistence.

nodes.name: ReversiGame
nodes.summary: Runs the Reversi game state machine, including move legality, pass logic, and AI responses.

nodes.name: BattleshipGame
nodes.summary: Runs the Battleship setup and battle loop across both human and AI participants.

nodes.name: Beep
nodes.summary: Procedural audio helper that synthesizes game sounds without shipping prerecorded media assets.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Web Audio primitives to synthesize tones, noise, and layered effects at runtime.

nodes.name: computeLocalHints
nodes.summary: Produces lightweight local hint suggestions when the full AI hint bridge is not available.

nodes.name: pickBestReversiMove
nodes.summary: Chooses a Reversi move using board weights and difficulty tuning, preferring high-value squares such as corners.

nodes.name: pickAIShot
nodes.summary: Dispatches to difficulty-specific Battleship targeting strategies to choose the next AI shot.

nodes.name: react-ui
nodes.summary: Cluster of React components and presentation files that render the app shell and each game interface.

nodes.name: ai-engine
nodes.summary: Cluster of AI search, rating, challenge, and plugin bridge files that support adaptive computer play.

nodes.name: adaptive-difficulty
nodes.summary: The app tunes computer strength over time by storing player skill and converting it into search depth and blunder behavior.

nodes.name: procedural-audio
nodes.summary: Sound feedback is generated at runtime with code rather than loaded from asset files, reducing bundle dependencies.

nodes.name: pwa-offline
nodes.summary: The app is packaged as an installable web application with manifest metadata and cached offline support.

edges.description: main.jsx mounts the App component.
main.jsx loads the global stylesheet.
App renders the home screen when no active game is selected.
App delegates Connect Four play to Game.jsx.
App delegates Reversi play to ReversiGame.jsx.
App delegates Battleship play to BattleshipGame.jsx.
setup.js loads the adapter bridge at startup.
setup.js loads the public plugin API.
setup.js loads DOM event wiring for plugin controls.
Game.jsx imports the Connect Four engine for win checks and move evaluation.
Game.jsx renders the board presentation component.
Game.jsx uses the shared result modal.
Game.jsx uses the shared turn timer.
Game.jsx shows a temporary win banner.
Game.jsx uses helper functions for board operations, hints, sounds, and sharing.
ReversiGame.jsx renders the Reversi board view.
ReversiGame.jsx shares the countdown timer component.
ReversiGame.jsx uses the shared win banner.
ReversiGame.jsx imports shared sound helpers.
ReversiGame.jsx relies on the Reversi rules helper library.
BattleshipGame.jsx renders the Battleship board component.
BattleshipGame.jsx uses the shared countdown timer.
BattleshipGame.jsx uses the shared win banner.
BattleshipGame.jsx imports shared sound helpers.
BattleshipGame.jsx relies on Battleship rules and targeting helpers.
Board.jsx imports shared board constants.
Board.jsx shows hazard badges for risky columns.
Board.jsx overlays the winning line when the game ends.
Home.jsx imports sound helpers for UI feedback.
WinBanner.jsx triggers shared audio feedback.
gameHelpers.js reuses core engine board functions.
coach.js calls the engine for legal moves, win checks, and best move search.
coach.js uses stored skill ratings to tune AI behavior.
challenge.js clones engine board structures while building daily seeds.
mm4-plugin.js exposes engine helpers on the public API.
mm4-plugin.js exposes AI move and hint helpers.
mm4-plugin.js exposes the daily challenge seed helper.
mm4-plugin.js exposes current skill accessors.
mm4-plugin-wire.js uses engine utilities while wiring plugin controls.
mm4-plugin-wire.js uses coach helpers to drive hint and AI actions.
mm4-plugin-wire.js imports daily challenge seed helpers.
engine.test.js imports engine functions directly.
reversi.test.js imports Reversi rules helpers directly.
battleship.test.js imports Battleship rule helpers directly.
engine.js defines the winner evaluator.
engine.js defines the bounded search entry point.
coach.js defines the AI move selector.
coach.js defines the hint computation helper.
rating.js defines the skill-update function.
App.jsx defines the top-level App component.
Game.jsx defines the Connect Four controller component.
ReversiGame.jsx defines the Reversi controller component.
BattleshipGame.jsx defines the Battleship controller component.
gameHelpers.js defines the Beep audio utility class.
gameHelpers.js defines the lightweight hint helper.
reversiHelpers.js defines the heuristic Reversi AI move picker.
battleshipHelpers.js defines the Battleship AI targeting dispatcher.
Game checks the engine winner function after moves and while evaluating tactical threats.
Game falls back to local hint generation when a global hint provider is absent.
ReversiGame delegates AI move selection to the Reversi helper library.
BattleshipGame asks the helper library for the next AI target cell.
aiMove uses the search engine to evaluate strong candidate moves.
computeHints checks for immediate tactical outcomes using the winner evaluator.
AI strength depends on skill state that is later updated after completed games.
coach.js implements adaptive difficulty behavior.
rating.js persists the player skill model behind adaptive difficulty.
gameHelpers.js is the implementation center for procedural sound effects.
Beep embodies the procedural audio concept in code.
index.html advertises the app as an installable web experience.
manifest.json configures installable PWA metadata.
sw.js implements offline caching and shell loading behavior.
The react-ui module includes the App screen controller.
The react-ui module includes the home screen.
The react-ui module includes the Connect Four controller.
The react-ui module includes the Reversi controller.
The react-ui module includes the Battleship controller.
The ai-engine module includes the Connect Four search engine.
The ai-engine module includes the coaching and hint layer.
The ai-engine module includes adaptive rating utilities.
The ai-engine module includes the daily challenge generator.
The AI module is organized around adaptive play behavior.
App.jsx sits inside the wider React UI module.
Connect Four gameplay depends on the AI engine module.
The engine is covered by dedicated Jest tests.
Reversi helper behavior is validated by the reversi test suite.
Battleship helpers are validated by the battleship test suite.
winner is directly exercised by engine.test.js.
bestMoveTimeboxed is directly exercised by engine.test.js.
pickBestReversiMove is validated by the Reversi test suite.
pickAIShot is validated across difficulty levels in the Battleship tests.
setup.js registers the service worker in production environments.
vite.config.js governs how the app is built and served around the PWA assets.
The README references the scripts and runtime dependencies declared in package.json.

layers.name: Entry And Config
layers.description: Bootstrap, build, metadata, and offline setup files that define how the app starts and ships.

layers.name: UI Layer
layers.description: React components and styling that present the home screen, game boards, overlays, and shared UI widgets.

layers.name: Game Logic
layers.description: Rule helpers and shared gameplay utilities for Connect Four, Reversi, Battleship, and synthesized feedback.

layers.name: AI Layer
layers.description: Search, rating, challenge seeding, analytics, and plugin bridge code that support computer play and integrations.

layers.name: Quality
layers.description: Automated tests and supporting project guidance that keep the core rule engines and AI behavior stable.

tour.title: Start With The Web Shell
tour.description: Read the HTML shell, package metadata, Vite config, and React bootstrap to see how the app is loaded, built, and registered as a PWA.

tour.title: Understand App-Level Routing
tour.description: Move to the App and Home components to understand how users choose a game, a mode, and a difficulty before gameplay begins.

tour.title: Follow The Connect Four Path
tour.description: Trace the main Connect Four loop through the Game controller, board component, helper utilities, and engine functions that decide wins and best moves.
tour.languageLesson: This path shows a common React pattern: UI components remain declarative while imperative game rules and search logic live in separate utility modules.

tour.title: Inspect Reversi State Transitions
tour.description: Open the Reversi controller and helper library to see legal move generation, disc flipping, pass logic, and heuristic AI move selection.

tour.title: Inspect Battleship Phase Management
tour.description: Read BattleshipGame and its helper library to understand setup, battle turns, shot resolution, and difficulty-based targeting.

tour.title: Study Adaptive AI
tour.description: Switch to the AI layer to see how coach.js, rating.js, and the core search engine collaborate to make the computer opponent stronger or weaker over time.
tour.languageLesson: This layer demonstrates how a policy function can wrap a search engine, injecting heuristics, stored preferences, and user-model state without modifying the engine itself.

tour.title: See Integration And Feedback Systems
tour.description: Review the plugin bridge, analytics hook, setup file, and procedural audio utility to understand how the app communicates outside React and enriches play.

tour.title: Finish With Validation
tour.description: End by reading the Jest suites that lock in winner detection, Reversi move generation, and Battleship rule correctness.
